name: La Cueva Santa y El Capurutxo — circular desde Fuente la Higuera
region_intro: Hiking around La Font De La Figuera offers a diverse landscape characterized by hills, ravines, and canyons. The region features a mix of Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Trails often follow natural watercourses or ascend to viewpoints, revealing the area's geological formations. The elevation changes across the routes contribute to a dynamic hiking experience. Best hiking trails around La Font De La Figuera The most popular hiking route is La Cueva Santa y El Capurutxo — circular desde Fuente la Higuera, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This circular route explores a cave and ascends to a local hill. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vallmelós Ravine Loop, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail navigates through a prominent ravine, offering views of its geological features. Local hikers also love the Capurutxo Trail, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around La Font De La Figuera is defined by its ravines, canyons, and hills, often featuring Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in La Font De La Figuera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore La Font De La Figuera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Font De La Figuera?
A: La Font De La Figuera offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 6 easy, 17 moderate, and 1 difficult option, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in La Font De La Figuera?
A: Yes, La Font De La Figuera has 6 easy hiking trails that are generally suitable for families. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, perfect for a relaxed outing. While specific family-friendly amenities like playgrounds directly on trails are not guaranteed, the easier paths offer a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging hiking trails in La Font De La Figuera?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, La Font De La Figuera features at least one difficult trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1321038949/capurutxo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capurutxo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a 9.6-mile (15.5 km) route with significant elevation changes, often taking over 5 hours to complete. These trails navigate hilly terrain and require good physical condition.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Font De La Figuera?
A: The hiking trails in La Font De La Figuera are characterized by a diverse landscape of hills, ravines, and canyons. You'll encounter a mix of Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests. Many trails follow natural watercourses or ascend to viewpoints, offering dynamic experiences and showcasing the area's unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Font De La Figuera?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323518458/la-cueva-santa-y-el-capurutxo-circular-desde-fuente-la-higuera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cueva Santa y El Capurutxo — circular desde Fuente la Higuera&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.0-mile (9.6 km) trail that explores a cave and ascends to a local hill. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323480589/rio-canoles-canyon-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cáñoles Canyon Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through the canyon.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Hiking in La Font De La Figuera offers opportunities to discover several natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2893417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt; gorge, the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6155567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cueva Santa&lt;/a&gt; cave, or the unique rock formation known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana de la Hoz&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bastida de les Alcusses&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Font De La Figuera?
A: The trails in La Font De La Figuera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic ravines and canyons, and the sense of exploration offered by routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323447188/vallmelos-ravine-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallmelós Ravine Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads?
A: While La Font De La Figuera is a smaller town, public transport options might be limited directly to all trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the starting points of the hikes. Many routes are accessible from the town itself or short drives away.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in La Font De La Figuera?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of La Font De La Figuera. For specific trailheads, you may find designated parking areas, especially for more popular routes. It's recommended to check the individual tour descriptions on komoot for details on parking availability near your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in La Font De La Figuera?
A: The Mediterranean climate of La Font De La Figuera makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for hiking. During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking then. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but some higher or more exposed trails might be chilly.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Font De La Figuera dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in La Font De La Figuera are suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse natural landscapes and open areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Check individual route details for any specific restrictions or recommendations regarding pets.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in La Font De La Figuera?
A: Given the varied terrain and Mediterranean climate, layered clothing is advisable. Sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential for navigating hills, ravines, and potentially uneven paths. Don't forget sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and plenty of water, especially during warmer months. A light jacket might be useful for cooler mornings or evenings.
tips: Deviating about 45 m in the NW direction you will find the Cueva Santa, a notable point on the route, it is located on one of the cliffs of the Capurutxo mountain range. It is a very visited cave, since its first section does not require any technical equipment.
The emblematic peak and cross of Capurutxo at 901 meters above sea level with spectacular views of the entire region.
